info.title: FDC+ Serial Drive Server API
info.description:
REST API for the Altair 8800 FDC+ Serial Drive Server web interface. Manages disk image serving, CP/M filesystem browsing, cassette audio, terminal serial connections, script replay, and XMODEM transfers.

## Socket.IO Events

The server also exposes real-time events via Socket.IO (not covered by OpenAPI).

### Server → Client

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | `ServerStatus` | Periodic server status broadcast (1s interval) |
| `terminal:data` | `number[]` | Incoming serial data as byte array |
| `terminal:status` | `TerminalStatus` | Terminal connection status change |
| `terminal:error` | `{message: string}` | Terminal error |
| `replay:progress` | `ReplayProgress` | Transfer progress update |
| `replay:status` | `{active, mode, progress}` | Transfer status on connect |

### Client → Server

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `request-status` | _(none)_ | Request current server status |
| `terminal:write` | `string` | Send keyboard input to terminal serial port |
| `terminal:control` | `{type, value}` | Set DTR/RTS control signals |
| `replay:start` | `ReplayStartParams` | Start raw replay or XMODEM send |
| `replay:cancel` | _(none)_ | Cancel active transfer |

tags.name: Snapshots
tags.description: Point-in-time disk image snapshots and rollback

tags.name: Clients
tags.description: Per-client drive-bay overrides and friendly names (multi-client serving)

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: Machine Profiles — declarative machines as versioned Primitives (Bitsby8)

tags.name: Instances
tags.description: Virtual S-100 Machine Instances — lifecycle + console I/O (Bitsby8)

lastIo.description: Epoch ms of most recent successful read/write; null if no I/O yet.

transient.description: Read-only image backed by a copy-on-write scratch; writes go to the scratch and the master stays pristine.

dirty.description: A transient-backed drive that has received at least one write since mount.

CardDefinition.description: A Catalog Card Definition (Bitsby8) — a versioned Primitive identified by name@version + digest.

digest.description: Content-addressed Identity (AD-8): sha256 over a JCS canonical Merkle manifest.

kind.description: Bus-ontology kind: S-100 board vs. component chip.

capabilities.description: Derived capability tags (from the manifest) — a browse/filter facet.

entry.description: Reference to the pre-built behavior module.

CardDetail.description: A card's datasheet (Bitsby8) — definition + footprint + Skills file + versions + used-by.

footprint.description: Default bus footprint (null if not derivable).

skills.description: Generated Skills file (human- and agent-readable markdown).

usedBy.description: Machine Profiles referencing this card (populated once Profiles exist).

MachineProfile.description: A declarative machine as a versioned Primitive (Bitsby8) — dual Identity (name@version + sha256) with immutable versions.

digest.description: Content digest; a change yields a new version + digest.

clock.description: Clock: { hz: number } or the string 'max'.

memory.description: Memory/ROM layout; a ROM region carries its bytes as base64 in `image`.

ProfileValidation.description: Define-time bus-collision validation of a Profile (Bitsby8). ok:true → runnable.

offenders.description: Colliding card instance ids (or memory region ids).

port.description: The colliding I/O port (kind port only).

claims.description: Each card's claimed bus footprint at its config.

InstanceSnapshot.description: An instance's disk/media snapshot (Bitsby8) — machine definition + per-drive disk state; execution state is not captured.

filename.description: Base image the drive was bound to.

MachineInstance.description: A virtual S-100 Machine Instance (Bitsby8) — a running or defined emulated machine.

profileRef.description: Where the profile came from (preset:<id> | inline).

transient.description: Memory-only; leaves no DB/splinter residue on destroy.

driver.description: Provenance — who drove it into existence.

headless.description: Running with no live console subscriber (agent-spun, unattended).

disks.description: Bound disks (operator mounts) with per-instance copy-on-write dirty state.

InstanceCreateRequest.description: Create a Machine Instance from a stored Profile ref, a preset id, or an inline MachineProfile (exactly one).

profileRef.description: Stored Machine Profile reference (name@version, or a bare name → latest).

profile.description: Inline MachineProfile (cpuKind, clock, resetVector, memory[], cards[], consoleCardId).

speed.description: Launch speed: Hz (e.g. 2000000 for authentic 2 MHz) or "max".

build.description: Debian revision derived at build time from git; null in dev builds.

commit.description: Short SHA of HEAD when the .deb was built; null in dev builds.

dirty.description: True when the working tree had uncommitted changes at build time.

builtAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC build timestamp; null in dev builds.

get.description: Returns terminal serial port connection state, device, config, and preferred settings.
Enumerates serial ports available for the terminal connection.

post.description: Open a serial port for the terminal connection.

config.description: Serial port configuration (baud, dataBits, stopBits, parity)

post.description: Close the terminal serial port connection.

put.description: Update serial port configuration (baud, data bits, stop bits, parity) for an open terminal connection.

get.summary: List snapshots for a disk image

200.description: Snapshots for the disk, newest first

post.summary: Create a snapshot of a disk image
post.description: Captures a point-in-time full copy of the disk. Allowed while the disk is mounted.

post.summary: Roll a disk image back to a snapshot
post.description: Overwrites the disk image with the snapshot's contents. Fails if the disk is mounted on any drive.

404.description: Disk image or snapshot not found

409.description: Disk is mounted on a drive

multiClientServing.description: Multi-client disk serving (concurrent clients + per-client splinters).

get.description: Enumerates serial ports on the host, including persistent device paths.

put.description: Change the serial device and baud rate for the primary FDC connection. Will close and reopen the port if needed.

get.description: Returns all files in the scripts directory with name and size.

post.description: Create a new script file. Fails if the file already exists.

get.description: Returns script metadata and content. Text files (.txt) include content; binary files return metadata only.

put.description: Overwrite the content of an existing script file.

post.description: Upload any file to the scripts directory. Max 1MB.
Start a raw text replay or XMODEM binary transfer of a script file over the terminal serial port.

interByteDelayMs.description: Delay between bytes in ms (raw mode)

interLineDelayMs.description: Delay between lines in ms (raw mode)

useCrc.description: Use CRC-16 instead of checksum (xmodem mode)

post.description: Cancel a running raw replay or XMODEM transfer.

get.description: Returns whether a transfer is active, its mode, and the last progress update.

get.summary: List Machine Profiles (latest version of each)

post.description: Create from a preset (`preset` + `name`) or from an explicit content body.

preset.description: Seed from a built-in preset (carries ROM + cards).

post.summary: Validate a (possibly unsaved) Profile for bus collisions
post.description: Define-time collision check (FR-8) — returns every collision (port/IRQ/memory) naming both offenders and the specific resource, plus each card's claimed footprint. `ok:true` means the Profile is runnable.
Sweeps each colliding card's base port for a collision-free value (FR-8). Returns the updated content plus any cards that couldn't be resolved.

post.summary: Import a Bitsby8 bundle into the Catalog (FR-24)
post.description: Registers the bundle's Machine Profile resolvable by Identity; requires its referenced cards to be present; reports (never overwrites) an already-present Identity.

name.description: Optional import name (defaults to the bundle name).

200.description: Imported — the new profile + card/warning report

get.summary: Get a Machine Profile by Identity (name@version)

put.summary: Save a change (writes a new version)
put.description: Applies a content patch and persists a NEW version with a new sha256; prior versions remain resolvable.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Delete just this version or every version of the name (default all).

get.summary: List a Profile's startup disk mounts
get.description: Which disk image each drive gets when a machine launches from this profile. Keyed by profile name (shared across versions).

put.summary: Bind a disk image to a Profile drive (mounts at startup)

delete.summary: Clear a Profile drive's startup disk

post.summary: Clone a Profile into an independent new one
Rename a Profile in place across all its versions
post.description: Re-keys every version from the current name to a new one, preserving full version history, notes and provenance (the content digest excludes the name, so nothing about the content changes). Also migrates the profile's name-keyed startup disks. Fails with 409 if a profile already exists under the target name.

200.description: The renamed profile (same version the id referred to)

get.summary: List all versions of a Profile name
Validate a stored Profile for bus collisions
Export a Profile to a self-describing bundle (FR-23)
get.description: A downloadable Bitsby8 bundle containing the Profile (ROM/media inline), its content Identity, and its referenced cards pinned by Identity. Carries no host-specific device paths.

post.summary: Burn a .bin/Intel HEX image into an EPROM card (FR-6)
post.description: Loads a base64 image into the EPROM card instance's ROM region, honoring the file's addresses or relocating from the region base. Persists a new Profile version with the burned bytes content-addressed.

image.description: base64-encoded file bytes (.bin or Intel HEX).

addressing.description: Honor file addresses ('file') or relocate to the region base ('base'). Default 'base'.

200.description: Burned — the new profile version + a burn summary

409.description: Card is not an EPROM/memory card

422.description: Image malformed or overflows the EPROM

delete.description: Drops the burned ROM override so the EPROM card reverts to empty. Persists a new Profile version when something was erased.

200.description: Erased (or a no-op if nothing was burned)

409.description: Card is not an EPROM/memory card

get.description: Ready-to-boot S-100 machine presets usable with POST /api/instances/transient.

post.summary: Define a persistent Machine Instance (not started)
Create and start a transient Machine Instance
post.description: Memory-only; boots immediately and leaves no residue on destroy. Mount a boot disk first.

get.summary: Get a Machine Instance by id

post.summary: Change a running instance's speed live (no restart)

200.description: The instance at its new speed

post.description: Captures the machine definition + each bound drive's disk state (execution state is out of scope).

post.summary: Restore a disk/media snapshot onto its instance
post.description: Stops the instance, writes the captured disks back, and restarts it (reproduces the disk state; the machine reboots).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Byte cursor from a prior read (default 0 = whole buffer)

get.summary: Read a running instance's GPIO ports (Story 5.8)
get.description: The parallel (GPIO) cards the machine exposes, with each port's direction and current latched output byte (drive LEDs / read a printer).

post.summary: Drive a GPIO card's input pins (Story 5.8)
post.description: Set the byte the CPU reads from a parallel card — sense switches. Applies to a running instance.

404.description: No such GPIO card on the instance

get.summary: Read a running instance's video displays (Story 5.9)
get.description: The video cards the machine exposes — each with a render descriptor (charGrid | bitmap) and a fresh frame (base64 of the video RAM the card maps). Poll for live output.

get.summary: Read a running instance's front-panel state (cockpit Phase 3)
get.description: CPU registers/flags, halted/running, and the address+data bus — for the Altair-style front panel.

post.description: run / stop / step / reset / examine / examNext / deposit / depNext. `value` is the switch register (address for examine, data for deposit). Returns the new state.

get.summary: List a running instance's keyboard cards (5.9)

200.description: Keyboard cards with pending key counts

post.summary: Inject keys into a keyboard card (5.9)
post.description: Feeds bytes to the guest's keyboard data port. Supply `byte`, `bytes`, or `text`; `cardId` targets one of several keyboard cards.

text.description: A string, sent one char at a time

cardId.description: Target keyboard card (when the machine has more than one)

get.description: Returns filenames of all disk images (.dsk, .img, .ima) in the disks directory.
Returns all disk images with file size, description, and notes.

post.description: Upload a disk image file (.dsk, .img, .ima). Max 10MB.
Create a copy of an existing disk image with a "-copy" suffix.
Create a new empty disk image in the specified format.

filename.description: Base filename (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens, periods, spaces)

format.description: Disk format: 8inch = 8-inch floppy (77 tracks, 330K), minidisk = 5.25" mini-disk (17 tracks, 75K), 8mb = 8 MB hard disk (1863 tracks, ~7.8 MB)

delete.description: Delete a disk image file. Fails if the image is currently mounted on any drive.

409.description: File is mounted on a drive

post.description: Erases every byte on the image and lays down a fresh MITS 8" sector layout. Fails if the disk is mounted.

format.description: Target format. Omit to derive from the current image size.

put.description: Set or update the description and notes for a disk image.

Rename a disk image file. Fails if the image is mounted on any drive, if the target filename already exists, or if the new name contains path separators / traversal sequences. Carries the description and notes over to the new filename.

newFilename.description: Desired new filename (basename only, no slashes)

409.description: Mounted, or target name already exists

get.summary: Get a disk image's read-only-write policy
get.description: Returns onReadonlyWrite: inherit (follow the global default), error (fail writes), or transient (back with copy-on-write scratch).

put.summary: Set a disk image's read-only-write policy

get.description: Returns server health status and current timestamp.
Returns serial connection state, disk serving state, drive statuses, and timestamp.

version.description: Upstream semver of the running build.

latestVersion.description: Newest release on GitHub, or null if the poll has not completed.

updateAvailable.description: True when latestVersion is strictly newer than version.

get.description:
Whitelisted past the auth middleware so the SPA can discover
what kind of authentication (if any) the daemon requires
before making its first authenticated call. Deliberately
reveals no state beyond the booleans — no key hint, no user
list, no session.

Two distinct booleans because the credentials are
independent:
- `loginRequired: true` when an `adminPassword` is set. The
  UI must prompt the operator to log in via
  `POST /api/auth/login`.
- `apiKeyRequired: true` when an `apiKey` is set. Machine
  clients (MCP over HTTP, curl) must pass
  `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>`.

`authRequired` is retained for one release as a legacy alias
equal to `loginRequired || apiKeyRequired` so the previous
AuthGate contract keeps working across an in-place upgrade.
Returns mount status, filename, read-only flag, head-loaded state, and current track for each drive.

post.description: Mount a disk image file onto the specified drive slot.

400.description: Missing filename or invalid drive ID

post.description: Unmount the disk image from the specified drive slot.

put.description: Toggle write protection on a mounted drive. May remount the file with the correct mode.

post.summary: Commit a transient drive's changes to its master image
post.description: Writes the copy-on-write scratch back onto the read-only master image. The drive stays mounted and transient. Fails if the drive is not transient-backed or the master is mounted on another drive.

400.description: Invalid drive ID or drive is not transient-backed

409.description: Master image is mounted on another drive

post.summary: Save a transient drive's changes as a snapshot
post.description: Captures the current copy-on-write scratch as a snapshot of the master image, without touching the master.

400.description: Invalid drive ID or drive is not transient-backed

post.description: Start the FDC server to serve disk images over serial. Opens serial port and begins listening for Altair commands.
Stop the FDC server and close the primary serial port.

get.description: Returns CP/M disk parameters, free space, file count, and mount status.

mounted.description: Drive number if mounted, false otherwise

get.description: List all files on the CP/M disk image.

post.summary: Upload file to CP/M disk image
post.description: Write a file into the CP/M filesystem on the disk image. Fails if the disk is currently mounted. Max 256KB.

400.description: No file uploaded or invalid filename

get.description: Extract and download a single file from the CP/M disk image. The cpmFile param format is "USER:NAME.EXT" (e.g. "0:ASM.COM").

parameters.name: cpmFile
parameters.description: CP/M file identifier in format USER:NAME.EXT

404.description: Disk image or CP/M file not found

delete.description: Remove a file from the CP/M filesystem on the disk image. Fails if the disk is currently mounted.

parameters.name: cpmFile
parameters.description: CP/M file identifier in format USER:NAME.EXT

404.description: Disk image or CP/M file not found

get.description: Returns current runtime configuration. Never includes `apiKey` — call `GET /api/config/status` to check whether it's set.

post.description: Legacy endpoint. Currently accepts `verbose` only. All other knobs must go through the per-section PUT endpoints and require a restart.

get.summary: JSON Schema for the config document
get.description: Emitted from the same Zod source of truth used to validate writes. Frontend renders field constraints from this — no duplicate schemas in the SPA.

Metadata the UI needs to decide whether the "Restart now" button
is available, whether saves will work, and how to detect a
successful restart (poll `startupEpoch`).

Returns the configured `apiKey` verbatim so an authenticated
operator can copy it back out at any time (password managers,
MCP client config, curl scripts). Unlike `adminPassword` — which
is bcrypt-hashed and unrecoverable — the API key is stored as
plaintext, so it can be read back. Protected by the same
session-or-Bearer auth as every other `/api/*` route, so only an
authenticated admin can reach it. Returns `{ apiKey: null }` when
no key is set.

200.description: The current API key (or null if unset)

post.summary: Gracefully exit so systemd relaunches the daemon
post.description:
Requires `?confirm=1` — the click path is destructive enough
that a stray XHR shouldn't take the daemon down. Returns 501
when the process isn't systemd-managed (dev / docker); the
UI should render a copy-paste command in that case.

202.description: Restart scheduled — poll GET /api/config/status until startupEpoch changes

post.summary: Gracefully stop the daemon (systemd leaves it down)
post.description:
Requires `?confirm=1`. Exits with a code the unit's
`RestartPreventExitStatus` treats as "do not relaunch", so the
service stops instead of restarting. Returns 501 when the process
isn't systemd-managed (dev / docker); the UI should render a
copy-paste command in that case. Starting it again is a manual
`systemctl start` from the host.

202.description: Shutdown scheduled — the daemon will stop and not relaunch

post.description:
Best-effort re-read of `verbose`, `debug`, and `logFile` from
the current on-disk config. Serial / web / GPIO changes still
need a full restart. Returns 200 with the set of fields that
actually took effect.

post.summary: Undo the last save by restoring `<config-file>.bak.1`
post.description:
Atomically swaps `.bak.1` in as the live config file and
shifts `.bak.2` → `.bak.1`, `.bak.3` → `.bak.2` so a second
rollback walks further back. Requires `?confirm=1`. Refused
in kiosk mode (`--config-readonly`).

200.description: Rolled back — includes the restored config and its new mtimeMs

409.description: No backup to roll back to

put.summary: Toggle MCP-over-HTTP for remote AI clients
put.description:
Persists `enableMcpHttp` and flips the runtime guard immediately —
no daemon restart. Refuses to enable when no API key is set (400).
Disabling drops any live MCP sessions.

Persists `enableWsTransport` and applies it live — no daemon
restart. When disabled, the /fdc-ws upgrade endpoint stops
accepting virtual FDC clients (403) and any client currently
connected is dropped. On by default: an absent field is treated
as enabled.

put.summary: Update the Web & API section (includes apiKey and adminPassword)
put.description:
Same shape as the other section PUTs, but two fields need
special handling:

- `apiKey` is a machine token; when non-null the value is
  written verbatim. Change is applied live (no restart) — the
  auth middleware reads apiKey via a runtime callback.
- `adminPassword` is bcrypt-hashed here before the write. Only
  the hash reaches disk; the plaintext never persists. Sending
  `null` clears the hash; omitting the key leaves it unchanged.
  Change is applied live (no restart).

Fields that still require a daemon restart (webPort, webHost,
enabling/disabling the web listener) are surfaced via
`restartRequired: true` in the response. When the patch only
touched auth fields, `restartRequired: false`.

get.summary: List known + connected clients with their effective drive bays

put.summary: Set a client's per-drive mount override

delete.summary: Clear a client's per-drive override (inherit global)
Forget a client (clear its overrides, splinters, and name)

post.summary: Commit a client's splinter back onto its master image (hot-swap)
post.description: Writes the client's private copy-on-write splinter for this drive back onto the shared master image, then hot-reloads every open handle (operator drives via a swap window, client sessions via resync) so live readers pick up the new contents. Client splinters re-attach by base name, keeping their own writes. Refused only when the base is held read-write by a live master-write path (an operator drive mounted read-write, or the connected master-write client), whose in-flight write would be clobbered.

200.description: Splinter committed to the master; response includes hotSwapped and the reloadedDrives list

409.description: Base is held read-write by a live master-write path

post.summary: Save a client's splinter as a snapshot of its master
post.description: Captures the client's current copy-on-write splinter for this drive as a snapshot of the master image, without touching the master or the splinter.

post.summary: Save a client's splinter as a new named disk image
post.description: Non-destructive publish — copies the client's copy-on-write splinter for this drive to a new named image in the disk library, without touching the live master. The name is suffixed (-2, -3, …) on collision; the extension defaults to the master's if omitted.

200.description: Saved; response includes the new filename

get.description: Returns Card Definitions in the Catalog (seed cards in Tier-1), each with Identity, type, maker, derived capabilities, and manifest — optionally filtered. The `facets` object lists the type/maker/capability values present across the full (unfiltered) set so a UI can render its filters from one call.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by card type (serial | floppy | memory | panel | other).

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: Filter to cards carrying this derived capability tag.

200.description: Filtered Card Definitions plus facet options

post.description: Create a Card Definition from a declarative behavior — a memory board (RAM/EPROM) or a CPU board — registered as source 'authored'. The host synthesizes its runtime bundle, so it seats and runs like a seed card.

get.summary: List the CPUs a Machine Profile can use (Story 5.3)
get.description: The processors the engine can run, derived from the seed CPU cards, for the Profile builder's CPU picker.

ref.description: Seed CPU card Identity, if any

get.summary: List peripheral endpoint types a card can bind to (Story 5.6)
get.description: The vocabulary of endpoints a card's far side connects to (terminal, disk, clock, gpio, display, socket), with what's wired today.

get.summary: List behavior kernels an authored I/O card can use (Story 5.7)
get.description: Trusted, parameterized device state machines (e.g. a serial UART) the host builds authored I/O cards from — no user code. Each names the peripheral endpoint it binds to.

delete.description: Removes an authored Card Definition. Refuses to delete built-in seed cards.

get.summary: Get a Card Definition by Identity

get.description: The Card Definition plus its default bus footprint (ports/IRQ), a generated Skills file (human- and agent-readable), the version list, and the used-by reverse index (populated once Machine Profiles exist).

post.summary: Validate a Card Instance config against the card's schema
post.description: Define-time validation (FR-7) — returns the defaults-filled `resolved` config plus any `errors` (each naming the parameter and the specific violation). Does not throw on invalid values.

get.description: Returns all cassette WAV files with size, description, and notes.

post.description: Upload a cassette WAV file. Max 100MB.

put.description: Set or update the description and notes for a cassette file.

get.description: Stream the cassette WAV file for client-side playback.

post.description: Play the cassette WAV file through the server's audio output. Stops any currently playing audio first.
Stop any currently playing cassette audio on the server.

post.summary: Verify the admin password and issue a session cookie
post.description:
Body `{ password }`. Compared (bcrypt) against the
`adminPassword` hash in the runtime config. Success sets
`fdcSession=<id>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/;
Max-Age=2592000` and returns 200. Bad password returns 401.
When no admin password is configured, returns 400 with
`code: LOGIN_NOT_CONFIGURED`.

Reads the session ID from the `fdcSession` cookie, drops it
from the in-memory store, and returns a cleared cookie.
Idempotent: called without a cookie or with a stale one it
still returns 200 so a "logout" click from a browser whose
session already expired doesn't error.

Requires the current session (auth middleware handles it).
Body `{ oldPassword, newPassword }` — the OLD password must
still verify against the stored hash even though the caller
is already authenticated. This is the mitigation against a
stolen cookie being used to permanently lock the operator
out. On success, the new hash lands in the override config
(via writePartialConfig, so backup rotation applies) and
every OTHER session is destroyed — the calling browser
stays logged in.
